rulings.Case Title: MUSEUM PLAZA LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ET AL VS. BERDING & WEIL LLP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance By Berding & Weil Llp
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Friday, February 28, 2025, Line 1. PLAINTIFFS MUSEUM PLAZA LLC, and WATERVIEW DEVELOPMENT LLC's Motion To Compel Compliance By Berding & Weil Llc. Plaintiffs Museum Plaza LLC and Waterview Development LLC move to compel defendant Berding & Weil LLP's compliance with the statement of compliance Defendant offered in its responses to Plaintiffs' requests for production. The motion is made pursuant to CCP Section 2031.320(a). That subsection provides, "[i]f a party filing a response to a demand for inspection . . . thereafter fails to permit the inspection . . . in accordance with that party's statement of compliance, the demanding party may move for an order compelling compliance." For the reasons below, the court grants the motion. (The court's complete ruling has been emailed to the parties.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: CAMAL EL SHERIFI ET AL VS. MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA VI ASSET MANAGEMENT, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One From Defendant Mercy Housing Management Group, Inc.; Request For Monetary Sanctions (Amended)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Friday, February 28, 2025, Line 5, PLAINTIFFS CAMAL EL SHERIFI, DAVID BAKER, LEA CURRY, MOHAMED SADI, MOHAMED ABID, MOUSLIH NEZHA, and CELSO BORGES's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One From Defendant Mercy Housing Management Group, Inc.; Request For Monetary Sanctions (Amended). Hearing required. The court is inclined to grant the motion in part, but a hearing is required to address Plaintiffs' citation to the purported case "Victory Woodworks, Inc. v. Triumph Construction Co. (2020) 50 Cal.App.5th 195," at page 4, lines 1 and 2, of Plaintiffs' supplemental brief. Plaintiffs should be prepared at the hearing to provide a copy of the case to court and counsel or to provide an explanation for why it is offered as authority. (The court's complete ruling has been emailed to the parties.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. rulings.Case Title: KATHLEEN JAMILIA MITCHELL VS. TOYOTA MOTOR SALES, U.S.A., INC., A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Plaintiff Kathleen Mitchell; Request For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Amanda S. Webber
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Friday, February 28, 2025, Line 6. DEFENDANT TOYOTA MOTOR SALES, U.S.A., INC's Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Plaintiff Kathleen Mitchell; Request For Monetary Sanctions. Defendant TOYOTA MOTOR SALES, U.S.A., INC.'s motion to compel the deposition of plaintiff Kathleen Mitchell and for sanctions is granted. Defendant has been attempting to depose plaintiff for months and plaintiff has not reasonably responded. (Webber Decl., pars. 1-9.) Plaintiff has acted without substantial justification. Plaintiff Kathleen Mitchell and/or her attorneys of record, Quill & Arrow, are sanctioned in the sum of $1,745 (the requested fees less the anticipated time to prepare for and appear at hearing, which the court does not find reasonable). The sanctions shall be paid and the deposition shall occur within 15 days of notice of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.
